Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x2DFA "MINDCONTROL-MASTERTRONIC-9000-2DFA" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 jsr $9000 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Mind Control (Mastertronic).prg"
Metadata.Notes:
This game has a BASIC program to provide instructions before the main game loads. However, as the main game code overwrites the custom charmap when loaded, the following steps were taken to retain the instructions: 1. Line 240 in the BASIC instructions program was changed from LOAD"" to a SYS call to a new machine code routine at $9100. 2. The first 1536 ($0600) bytes of the custom charmap used in the instructions at $3000 were dumped (this includes the characters for the Mastertronic logo etc). 3. The game was dumped at the start address of $2DFA. 4. The 256 bytes at $0CC0 were copied to $9900. 5. The 1536 ($0600) bytes at $3000 were copied to $9200. 6. A new routine was placed at $9000 which sets up the correct VICII settings and necessary BASIC pointers and runs the instructions program. 7. This routine also disables the RUN/STOP key by modifying the STOP vector by setting $E1 at $0328. 8. Once the instructions program has finished, the added SYS call jumps to the new routine at $9100, which: a) restores the 1536 ($0600) bytes of data from $9200 back to $3000 b) restores the 256 bytes of data from $9900 back to $0CC0 (overwriting the end section of the BASIC instructions program) c) jumps to the games entry point at $2DFA
Note: to dump the instructions program, it is important to set a breakpoint at $A7AE, because if it's dumped whilst running, the first part of the program has been overwritten.
